verses.latin_vulgate: Mulier autem quædam de uxoribus prophetarum clamabat ad Eliseum, dicens : Servus tuus vir meus mortuus est, et tu nosti quia servus tuus fuit timens Dominum : et ecce creditor venit ut tollat duos filios meos ad serviendum sibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִשָּׁ֣ה אַחַ֣ת מִנְּשֵׁ֣י בְנֵֽי־הַ֠נְּבִיאִ֠ים צָעֲקָ֨ה אֶל־אֱלִישָׁ֜ע לֵאמֹ֗ר עַבְדְּךָ֤ אִישִׁי֙ מֵ֔ת וְאַתָּ֣ה יָדַ֔עְתָּ כִּ֣י עַבְדְּךָ֔ הָיָ֥ה יָרֵ֖א אֶת־יְהֹוָ֑ה וְהַ֨נֹּשֶׁ֔ה בָּ֗א לָקַ֜חַת אֶת־שְׁנֵ֧י יְלָדַ֛י ל֖וֹ לַעֲבָדִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now a certain woman of the wives of the prophets, cried to Eliseus, saying: Thy servant, my husband, is dead, and thou knowest that thy servant was one that feared God, and behold the creditor is come to take away my two sons to serve him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja yksi vaimo prophetain poikain emäntiä huusi Elisan tykö ja sanoi: sinun palvelias minun mieheni kuoli: niin sinä tiedät sen, että sinun palvelias pelkäsi Herraa: nyt tuli velkamies, ja tahtoo ottaa kaksi minun poikaani orjaksensa.
verses.king_james: Now there cried a certain woman of the wives of the sons of the prophets unto Elisha, saying, Thy servant my husband is dead; and thou knowest that thy servant did fear the Lord: and the creditor is come to take unto him my two sons to be bondmen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui dixit Eliseus : Quid vis ut faciam tibi ? dic mihi, quid habes in domo tua ? At illa respondit : Non habeo ancilla tua quidquam in domo mea, nisi parum olei quo ungar.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר אֵלֶ֤יהָ אֱלִישָׁע֙ מָ֣ה אֶֽעֱשֶׂה־לָּ֔ךְ הַגִּ֣ידִי לִ֔י מַה־יֶּשׁ־[לָ֖ךְ] (לכי) בַּבָּ֑יִת וַתֹּ֗אמֶר אֵ֣ין לְשִׁפְחָתְךָ֥ כֹל֙ בַּבַּ֔יִת כִּ֖י אִם־אָס֥וּךְ שָֽׁמֶן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Eliseus said to her: What wilt thou have me do for thee? Tell me, what hast thou in thy house? And she answered: I, thy handmaid, have nothing in my house but a little oil, to anoint me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Elisa sanoi hänelle: mitä minun pitää tekemän sinulle? sano minulle, mitä sinun huoneessas on? Hän sanoi: sinun piiallas ei ole muuta mitään kuin yksi öljyastia.
verses.king_james: And Elisha said unto her, What shall I do for thee? tell me, what hast thou in the house? And she said, Thine handmaid hath not any thing in the house, save a pot of oil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui ait : Vade, pete mutuo ab omnibus vicinis tuis vasa vacua non pauca,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֗אמֶר לְכִ֨י שַׁאֲלִי־לָ֤ךְ כֵּלִים֙ מִן־הַח֔וּץ מֵאֵ֖ת כׇּל־[שְׁכֵנָ֑יִךְ] (שכנכי) כֵּלִ֥ים רֵקִ֖ים אַל־תַּמְעִֽיטִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to her: Go, borrow of all thy neighbours empty vessels, not a few.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi: mene ja ano koko kyläkunnaltas astioita, tyhjiä astioita niin monta kuin saat,
verses.king_james: Then he said, Go, borrow thee vessels abroad of all thy neighbours, even empty vessels; borrow not a few.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ingredere, et claude ostium tuum cum intrinsecus fueris tu, et filii tui : et mitte inde in omnia vasa hæc, et cum plena fuerint, tolles.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבָ֗את וְסָגַ֤רְתְּ הַדֶּ֙לֶת֙ בַּעֲדֵ֣ךְ וּבְעַד־בָּנַ֔יִךְ וְיָצַ֕קְתְּ עַ֥ל כׇּל־הַכֵּלִ֖ים הָאֵ֑לֶּה וְהַמָּלֵ֖א תַּסִּֽיעִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And go in, and shut thy door, when thou art within, and thy sons: and pour out thereof into all those vessels: and when they are full, take them away.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja mene sisälle, ja sulje ovi sinun ja sinun poikais perään, ja kaada jokaiseen niihin astioihin: ja koskas olet ne täyttänyt, niin anna ne ottaa pois.
verses.king_james: And when thou art come in, thou shalt shut the door upon thee and upon thy sons, and shalt pour out into all those vessels, and thou shalt set aside that which is full.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ivit itaque mulier, et clausit ostium super se, et super filios suos : illi offerebant vasa, et illa infundebat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֵּ֙לֶךְ֙ מֵֽאִתּ֔וֹ וַתִּסְגֹּ֣ר הַדֶּ֔לֶת בַּעֲדָ֖הּ וּבְעַ֣ד בָּנֶ֑יהָ הֵ֛ם מַגִּישִׁ֥ים אֵלֶ֖יהָ וְהִ֥יא (מיצקת) [מוֹצָֽקֶת]׃
verses.douay_rheims: So the woman went, and shut the door upon her, and upon her sons: they brought her the vessels, and she poured in.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän meni pois hänen tyköänsä, sulki oven jälkeensä ja poikainsa jälkeen. He kantoivat astiat hänen eteensä, ja hän kaasi niihin.
verses.king_james: So she went from him, and shut the door upon her and upon her sons, who brought the vessels to her; and she poured out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque plena fuissent vasa, dixit ad filium suum : Affer mihi adhuc vas. Et ille respondit : Non habeo. Stetitque oleum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י ׀ כִּמְלֹ֣את הַכֵּלִ֗ים וַתֹּ֤אמֶר אֶל־בְּנָהּ֙ הַגִּ֨ישָׁה אֵלַ֥י עוֹד֙ כֶּ֔לִי וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֵלֶ֔יהָ אֵ֥ין ע֖וֹד כֶּ֑לִי וַֽיַּעֲמֹ֖ד הַשָּֽׁמֶן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when the vessels were full, she said to her son: Bring me yet a vessel. And he answered: I have no more. And the oil stood.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin astiat olivat täytetyt, sanoi hän pojallensa: tuo minulle vielä astia. Hän sanoi hänelle: ei ole täällä enää astiaa; niin öljy seisahti.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when the vessels were full, that she said unto her son, Bring me yet a vessel. And he said unto her, There is not a vessel more. And the oil stayed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit autem illa, et indicavit homini Dei. Et ille : Vade, inquit, vende oleum, et redde creditori tuo : tu autem, et filii tui vivite de reliquo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתָּבֹ֗א וַתַּגֵּד֙ לְאִ֣ישׁ הָאֱלֹהִ֔ים וַיֹּ֗אמֶר לְכִי֙ מִכְרִ֣י אֶת־הַשֶּׁ֔מֶן וְשַׁלְּמִ֖י אֶת־[נִשְׁיֵ֑ךְ] (נשיכי) וְאַ֣תְּ (בניכי) [וּבָנַ֔יִךְ] תִּֽחְיִ֖י בַּנּוֹתָֽר׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And she came, and told the man of God. And he said: Go, sell the oil, and pay thy creditor: and thou and thy sons live of the rest.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän tuli ja ilmoitti sen Jumalan miehelle. Hän sanoi: mene ja myy öljy, ja maksa velkamiehes; mutta sinä ja sinun poikas eläkäät jääneestä.
verses.king_james: Then she came and told the man of God. And he said, Go, sell the oil, and pay thy debt, and live thou and thy children of the rest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Facta est autem quædam dies, et transibat Eliseus per Sunam : erat autem ibi mulier magna, quæ tenuit eum ut comederet panem : cumque frequenter inde transiret, divertebat ad eam ut comederet panem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֨י הַיּ֜וֹם וַיַּעֲבֹ֧ר אֱלִישָׁ֣ע אֶל־שׁוּנֵ֗ם וְשָׁם֙ אִשָּׁ֣ה גְדוֹלָ֔ה וַתַּחֲזֶק־בּ֖וֹ לֶאֱכׇל־לָ֑חֶם וַֽיְהִי֙ מִדֵּ֣י עׇבְר֔וֹ יָסֻ֥ר שָׁ֖מָּה לֶאֱכׇל־לָֽחֶם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And there was a day when Eliseus passed by Sunam: now there was a great woman there, who detained him to eat bread: and as he passed often that way, he turned into her house to eat bread.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja se tapahtui siihen aikaan, että Elisa meni Sunemiin, ja siellä oli varallinen vaimo, joka vaati häntä syömään tykönänsä. Ja niin usein kuin hän sen kautta vaelsi, poikkesi hän sinne syömään leipää.
verses.king_james: And it fell on a day, that Elisha passed to Shunem, where was a great woman; and she constrained him to eat bread. And so it was, that as oft as he passed by, he turned in thither to eat bread.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ dixit ad virum suum : Animadverto quod vir Dei sanctus est iste, qui transit per nos frequenter.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אֶל־אִישָׁ֔הּ הִנֵּה־נָ֣א יָדַ֔עְתִּי כִּ֛י אִ֥ישׁ אֱלֹהִ֖ים קָד֣וֹשׁ ה֑וּא עֹבֵ֥ר עָלֵ֖ינוּ תָּמִֽיד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she said to her husband: I perceive that this is a holy man of God, who often passeth by us.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi miehellensä: katso, minä ymmärrän tämän Jumalan miehen olevan pyhän, joka tässä meidän kauttamme aina vaeltaa:
verses.king_james: And she said unto her husband, Behold now, I perceive that this is an holy man of God, which passeth by us continually.

verses.latin_vulgate: Faciamus ergo ei cœnaculum parvum, et ponamus ei in eo lectulum, et mensam, et sellam, et candelabrum, ut cum venerit ad nos, maneat ibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נַעֲשֶׂה־נָּ֤א עֲלִיַּת־קִיר֙ קְטַנָּ֔ה וְנָשִׂ֨ים ל֥וֹ שָׁ֛ם מִטָּ֥ה וְשֻׁלְחָ֖ן וְכִסֵּ֣א וּמְנוֹרָ֑ה וְהָיָ֛ה בְּבֹא֥וֹ אֵלֵ֖ינוּ יָס֥וּר שָֽׁמָּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let us, therefore, make him a little chamber, and put a little bed in it for him, and a table, and a stool, and a candlestick, that when he cometh to us he may abide there.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tehkäämme siis tähän ylistupa, ja pankaamme hänelle sinne vuode, pöytä, istuin ja kynttiläjalka, että hän sinne menis, kuin hän meidän tykömme tulee.
verses.king_james: Let us make a little chamber, I pray thee, on the wall; and let us set for him there a bed, and a table, and a stool, and a candlestick: and it shall be, when he cometh to us, that he shall turn in thither.

verses.latin_vulgate: Facta est ergo dies quædam, et veniens divertit in cœnaculum, et requievit ibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֥י הַיּ֖וֹם וַיָּ֣בֹא שָׁ֑מָּה וַיָּ֥סַר אֶל־הָעֲלִיָּ֖ה וַיִּשְׁכַּב־שָֽׁמָּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now, there was a certain day, when he came, and turned into the chamber, and rested there.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin tapahtui siihen aikaan, että hän tuli sinne; ja hän meni ylistupaan ja makasi siellä.
verses.king_james: And it fell on a day, that he came thither, and he turned into the chamber, and lay there.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ad Giezi puerum suum : Voca Sunamitidem istam. Qui cum vocasset eam, et illa stetisset coram eo,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אֶל־גֵּיחֲזִ֣י נַעֲר֔וֹ קְרָ֖א לַשּׁוּנַמִּ֣ית הַזֹּ֑את וַיִּ֨קְרָא־לָ֔הּ וַֽתַּעֲמֹ֖ד לְפָנָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to Giezi, his servant: Call this Sunamitess. And when he had called her, and she stood before him,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi palveliallensa Gehatsille: kutsu Sunemin vaimo tänne. Ja kuin hän oli kutsunut hänen, astui hän hänen eteensä.
verses.king_james: And he said to Gehazi his servant, Call this Shunammite. And when he had called her, she stood before him.

verses.latin_vulgate: dixit ad puerum suum : Loquere ad eam : Ecce, sedule in omnibus ministrasti nobis : quid vis ut faciam tibi ? numquid habes negotium, et vis ut loquar regi, sive principi militiæ ? Quæ respondit : In medio populi mei habito.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר ל֗וֹ אֱמׇר־נָ֣א אֵלֶ֘יהָ֮ הִנֵּ֣ה חָרַ֣דְתְּ ׀ אֵלֵ֘ינוּ֮ אֶת־כׇּל־הַחֲרָדָ֣ה הַזֹּאת֒ מֶ֚ה לַעֲשׂ֣וֹת לָ֔ךְ הֲיֵ֤שׁ לְדַבֶּר־לָךְ֙ אֶל־הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ א֖וֹ אֶל־שַׂ֣ר הַצָּבָ֑א וַתֹּ֕אמֶר בְּת֥וֹךְ עַמִּ֖י אָנֹכִ֥י יֹשָֽׁבֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: He said to his servant: Say to her: Behold, thou hast diligently served us in all things; what wilt thou have me to do for thee? Hast thou any business, and wilt thou, that I speak to the king, or to the general of the army? And she answered: I dwell in the midst of my own people.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin hän sanoi hänelle: sano hänelle: katso, sinä olet tehnyt meille kaiken tämän palveluksen, mitä minun pitää sinulle tekemän? Onko sinulla asiaa kuninkaan taikka sodanpäämiehen tykö? Hän sanoi: minä asun keskellä minun kansaani.
verses.king_james: And he said unto him, Say now unto her, Behold, thou hast been careful for us with all this care; what is to be done for thee? wouldest thou be spoken for to the king, or to the captain of the host? And she answered, I dwell among mine own people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait : Quid ergo vult ut faciam ei ? Dixitque Giezi : Ne quæras : filium enim non habet, et vir ejus senex est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֕אמֶר וּמֶ֖ה לַעֲשׂ֣וֹת לָ֑הּ וַיֹּ֣אמֶר גֵּיחֲזִ֗י אֲבָ֛ל בֵּ֥ן אֵֽין־לָ֖הּ וְאִישָׁ֥הּ זָקֵֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said: What will she then that I do for her? And Giezi said: Do not ask, for she hath no son, and her husband is old.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi hänelle: mitästä siis hänelle pitäis tehtämän? Gehatsi sanoi: ei ole hänellä poikaa, ja hänen miehensä on vanha.
verses.king_james: And he said, What then is to be done for her? And Gehazi answered, Verily she hath no child, and her husband is old.

verses.latin_vulgate: Præcepit itaque ut vocaret eam : quæ cum vocata fuisset, et stetisset ante ostium,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֖אמֶר קְרָא־לָ֑הּ וַיִּ֨קְרָא־לָ֔הּ וַֽתַּעֲמֹ֖ד בַּפָּֽתַח׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then he bid him call her. And when she was called, and stood before the door,
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi: kutsu häntä; ja kuin hän meni häntä kutsumaan, niin hän tuli omissa.
verses.king_james: And he said, Call her. And when he had called her, she stood in the door.

verses.latin_vulgate: dixit ad eam : In tempore isto, et in hac eadem hora, si vita comes fuerit, habebis in utero filium. At illa respondit : Noli quæso, domine mi vir Dei, noli mentiri ancillæ tuæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֗אמֶר לַמּוֹעֵ֤ד הַזֶּה֙ כָּעֵ֣ת חַיָּ֔ה (אתי) [אַ֖תְּ] חֹבֶ֣קֶת בֵּ֑ן וַתֹּ֗אמֶר אַל־אֲדֹנִי֙ אִ֣ישׁ הָאֱלֹהִ֔ים אַל־תְּכַזֵּ֖ב בְּשִׁפְחָתֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He said to her: At this time, and this same hour, if life be in company, thou shalt have a son in thy womb. But she answered: Do not, I beseech thee, my lord, thou man of God, do not lie to thy handmaid.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi: tähän määrättyyn aikaan vuoden perästä pitää sinun syleilemän poikaa. Hän sanoi: ei, minun herrani, sinä Jumalan mies, älä vääryttele piikas edessä!
verses.king_james: And he said, About this season, according to the time of life, thou shalt embrace a son. And she said, Nay, my lord, thou man of God, do not lie unto thine handmaid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et concepit mulier, et peperit filium in tempore, et in hora eadem, qua dixerat Eliseus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּ֥הַר הָאִשָּׁ֖ה וַתֵּ֣לֶד בֵּ֑ן לַמּוֹעֵ֤ד הַזֶּה֙ כָּעֵ֣ת חַיָּ֔ה אֲשֶׁר־דִּבֶּ֥ר אֵלֶ֖יהָ אֱלִישָֽׁע׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the woman conceived, and brought forth a son in the time, and at the same hour that Eliseus had said.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja vaimo tuli raskaaksi ja synnytti pojan määrättyyn aikaan vuoden perästä, niinkuin Elisa oli hänelle sanonut.
verses.king_james: And the woman conceived, and bare a son at that season that Elisha had said unto her, according to the time of life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Crevit autem puer : et cum esset quædam dies, et egressus isset ad patrem suum, ad messores,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּגְדַּ֖ל הַיָּ֑לֶד וַיְהִ֣י הַיּ֔וֹם וַיֵּצֵ֥א אֶל־אָבִ֖יו אֶל־הַקֹּצְרִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the child grew. And on a certain day, when he went out to his father to the reapers,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin lapsi jo isoksi tuli, niin tapahtui, että hän meni ulos isänsä ja elonleikkaajain tykö.
verses.king_james: And when the child was grown, it fell on a day, that he went out to his father to the reapers.

verses.latin_vulgate: ait patri suo : Caput meum doleo, caput meum doleo. At ille dixit puero : Tolle, et duc eum ad matrem suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר אֶל־אָבִ֖יו רֹאשִׁ֣י ׀ רֹאשִׁ֑י וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אֶל־הַנַּ֔עַר שָׂאֵ֖הוּ אֶל־אִמּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He said to his father: My head acheth, my head acheth. But he said to his servant. Take him, and carry him to his mother.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi isällensä: voi-minun päätäni, minun päätäni! Ja hän sanoi palveliallensa: kanna häntä äitinsä tykö!
verses.king_james: And he said unto his father, My head, my head. And he said to a lad, Carry him to his mother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui cum tulisset, et duxisset eum ad matrem suam, posuit eum illa super genua sua usque ad meridiem, et mortuus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּ֨שָּׂאֵ֔הוּ וַיְבִיאֵ֖הוּ אֶל־אִמּ֑וֹ וַיֵּ֧שֶׁב עַל־בִּרְכֶּ֛יהָ עַד־הַֽצׇּהֳרַ֖יִם וַיָּמֹֽת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when he had taken him, and brought him to his mother, she sat him on her knees, until noon, and then he died.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän otti hänen ja vei äitinsä tykö; ja hän istui hänen helmassansa puolipäivään asti, ja kuoli.
verses.king_james: And when he had taken him, and brought him to his mother, he sat on her knees till noon, and then died.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ascendit autem, et collocavit eum super lectulum hominis Dei, et clausit ostium : et egressa,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּ֙עַל֙ וַתַּשְׁכִּבֵ֔הוּ עַל־מִטַּ֖ת אִ֣ישׁ הָאֱלֹהִ֑ים וַתִּסְגֹּ֥ר בַּעֲד֖וֹ וַתֵּצֵֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she went up, and laid him upon the bed of the man of God, and shut the door: and going out,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän nousi ja laski hänen Jumalan miehen vuoteelle, läksi ulos ja sulki oven,
verses.king_james: And she went up, and laid him on the bed of the man of God, and shut the door upon him, and went out.

verses.latin_vulgate: vocavit virum suum, et ait : Mitte mecum, obsecro, unum de pueris, et asinam, ut excurram usque ad hominem Dei, et revertar.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּקְרָא֮ אֶל־אִישָׁהּ֒ וַתֹּ֗אמֶר שִׁלְחָ֨ה נָ֥א לִי֙ אֶחָ֣ד מִן־הַנְּעָרִ֔ים וְאַחַ֖ת הָאֲתֹנ֑וֹת וְאָר֛וּצָה עַד־אִ֥ישׁ הָאֱלֹהִ֖ים וְאָשֽׁוּבָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: She called her husband, and said: Send with me, I beseech thee, one of thy servants, and an ass, that I may run to the man of God, and come again.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kutsui miehensä ja sanoi: lähetä minun tyköni yksi palvelioista ja yksi aaseista, sillä minä menen Jumalan miehen tykö ja palajan.
verses.king_james: And she called unto her husband, and said, Send me, I pray thee, one of the young men, and one of the asses, that I may run to the man of God, and come again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui ait illi : Quam ob causam vadis ad eum ? hodie non sunt calendæ, neque sabbatum. Quæ respondit : Vadam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֗אמֶר מַ֠דּ֠וּעַ (אתי) [אַ֣תְּ] (הלכתי) [הֹלֶ֤כֶת] אֵלָיו֙ הַיּ֔וֹם לֹא־חֹ֖דֶשׁ וְלֹ֣א שַׁבָּ֑ת וַתֹּ֖אמֶר שָׁלֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to her: Why dost thou go to him? to day is neither new moon nor sabbath. She answered: I will go.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi: miksi sinä hänen tykönsä menet tänäpänä, sillä ei nyt ole uusi kuu taikka sabbati? Hän sanoi: rauha.
verses.king_james: And he said, Wherefore wilt thou go to him to day? it is neither new moon, nor sabbath. And she said, It shall be well.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stravitque asinam, et præcepit puero : Mina, et propera : ne mihi moram facias in eundo : et hoc age quod præcipio tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽתַּחֲבֹשׁ֙ הָאָת֔וֹן וַתֹּ֥אמֶר אֶֽל־נַעֲרָ֖הּ נְהַ֣ג וָלֵ֑ךְ אַל־תַּעֲצׇר־לִ֣י לִרְכֹּ֔ב כִּ֖י אִם־אָמַ֥רְתִּי לָֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she saddled an ass, and commanded her servant: Drive, and make haste, make no stay in going: And do that which I bid thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän satuloitsi aasin ja sanoi palveliallensa: aja vahvasti, älä viivytä ajamasta, muutoin kuin minä sanon sinulle.
verses.king_james: Then she saddled an ass, and said to her servant, Drive, and go forward; slack not thy riding for me, except I bid thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Profecta est igitur, et venit ad virum Dei in montem Carmeli : cumque vidisset eam vir Dei e contra, ait ad Giezi puerum suum : Ecce Sunamitis illa.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֵּ֗לֶךְ וַתָּבֹ֛א אֶל־אִ֥ישׁ הָאֱלֹהִ֖ים אֶל־הַ֣ר הַכַּרְמֶ֑ל וַ֠יְהִ֠י כִּרְא֨וֹת אִישׁ־הָאֱלֹהִ֤ים אֹתָהּ֙ מִנֶּ֔גֶד וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אֶל־גֵּיחֲזִ֣י נַעֲר֔וֹ הִנֵּ֖ה הַשּׁוּנַמִּ֥ית הַלָּֽז׃
verses.douay_rheims: So she went forward, and came to the man of God, to mount Carmel: and when the man of God saw her coming towards, he said to Giezi, his servant: Behold that Sunamitess.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja niin hän meni pois ja tuli Jumalan miehen tykö Karmelin vuorelle. Kuin Jumalan mies näki hänen tulevan, sanoi hän palveliallensa Gehatsille: katso, se on Sunemin vaimo.
verses.king_james: So she went and came unto the man of God to mount Carmel. And it came to pass, when the man of God saw her afar off, that he said to Gehazi his servant, Behold, yonder is that Shunammite:

verses.latin_vulgate: Vade ergo in occursum ejus, et dic ei : Recte ne agitur circa te, et circa virum tuum, et circa filium tuum ? Quæ respondit : Recte.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַתָּה֮ רֽוּץ־נָ֣א לִקְרָאתָהּ֒ וֶאֱמׇר־לָ֗הּ הֲשָׁל֥וֹם לָ֛ךְ הֲשָׁל֥וֹם לְאִישֵׁ֖ךְ הֲשָׁל֣וֹם לַיָּ֑לֶד וַתֹּ֖אמֶר שָׁלֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Go, therefore, to meet her, and say to her: Is all well with thee, and with thy husband, and with thy son? And she answered: Well.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin juokse nyt häntä vastaan ja sano hänelle: oletkos rauhassa, ja sinun miehes ja sinun poikas? Hän vastasi: rauhassa.
verses.king_james: Run now, I pray thee, to meet her, and say unto her, Is it well with thee? is it well with thy husband? is it well with the child? And she answered, It is well.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque venisset ad virum Dei in montem, apprehendit pedes ejus : et accessit Giezi ut amoveret eam. Et ait homo Dei : Dimitte illam : anima enim ejus in amaritudine est, et Dominus celavit a me, et non indicavit mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתָּבֹ֞א אֶל־אִ֤ישׁ הָאֱלֹהִים֙ אֶל־הָהָ֔ר וַֽתַּחֲזֵ֖ק בְּרַגְלָ֑יו וַיִּגַּ֨שׁ גֵּיחֲזִ֜י לְהׇדְפָ֗הּ וַיֹּ֩אמֶר֩ אִ֨ישׁ הָאֱלֹהִ֤ים הַרְפֵּה־לָהּ֙ כִּֽי־נַפְשָׁ֣הּ מָֽרָה־לָ֔הּ וַֽיהֹוָה֙ הֶעְלִ֣ים מִמֶּ֔נִּי וְלֹ֥א הִגִּ֖יד לִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when she came to the man of God, to the mount, she caught hold on his feet: and Giezi came to remove her. And the man of God said: Let her alone for her soul is in anguish, and the Lord hath hid it from me, and hath not told me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kuin hän tuli vuorelle Jumalan miehen tykö, tarttui hän hänen jalkoihinsa. Ja Gehatsi meni sysäämään häntä pois. Mutta Jumalan mies sanoi: anna hänen olla, sillä hänen sielunsa on murheellinen, ja Herra on minulta sen salannut ja ei ilmoittanut minulle.
verses.king_james: And when she came to the man of God to the hill, she caught him by the feet: but Gehazi came near to thrust her away. And the man of God said, Let her alone; for her soul is vexed within her: and the Lord hath hid it from me, and hath not told me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ dixit illi : Numquid petivi filium a domino meo ? numquid non dixi tibi : Ne illudas me ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֕אמֶר הֲשָׁאַ֥לְתִּי בֵ֖ן מֵאֵ֣ת אֲדֹנִ֑י הֲלֹ֣א אָמַ֔רְתִּי לֹ֥א תַשְׁלֶ֖ה אֹתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she said to him: Did I ask a son of my lord? did I not say to thee: Do not deceive me?
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi: anoinko minä poikaa minun herraltani? Enkö minä sanonut: älä minua petä!
verses.king_james: Then she said, Did I desire a son of my lord? did I not say, Do not deceive me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ille ait ad Giezi : Accinge lumbos tuos, et tolle baculum meum in manu tua, et vade. Si occurrerit tibi homo, non salutes eum : et si salutaverit te quispiam, non respondeas illi : et pones baculum meum super faciem pueri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר לְגֵיחֲזִ֜י חֲגֹ֣ר מׇתְנֶ֗יךָ וְקַ֨ח מִשְׁעַנְתִּ֣י בְיָדְךָ֮ וָלֵךְ֒ כִּי־תִמְצָ֥א אִישׁ֙ לֹ֣א תְבָרְכֶ֔נּוּ וְכִֽי־יְבָרֶכְךָ֥ אִ֖ישׁ לֹ֣א תַעֲנֶ֑נּוּ וְשַׂמְתָּ֥ מִשְׁעַנְתִּ֖י עַל־פְּנֵ֥י הַנָּֽעַר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then he said to Giezi: Gird up thy loins, and take my staff in thy hand, and go. If any man meet thee, salute him not: and if any man salute thee, answer him not: and lay my staff upon the face of the child.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi Gehatsille: vyötä sinun kupees ja ota minun sauvani kätees, ja mene matkaas: jos joku sinun kohtaa, niin älä tervehdi häntä, ja jos joku sinua tervehtii, niin älä vastaa häntä: ja laske minun sauvani pojan kasvoille.
verses.king_james: Then he said to Gehazi, Gird up thy loins, and take my staff in thine hand, and go thy way: if thou meet any man, salute him not; and if any salute thee, answer him not again: and lay my staff upon the face of the child.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro mater pueri ait : Vivit Dominus, et vivit anima tua, non dimittam te. Surrexit ergo, et secutus est eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אֵ֣ם הַנַּ֔עַר חַי־יְהֹוָ֥ה וְחֵֽי־נַפְשְׁךָ֖ אִם־אֶעֶזְבֶ֑ךָּ וַיָּ֖קׇם וַיֵּ֥לֶךְ אַחֲרֶֽיהָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the mother of the child said: As the Lord liveth, and as thy soul liveth, I will not leave thee. He arose, therefore, and followed her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta pojan äiti sanoi: niin totta kuin Herra elää ja sinun sielus elää, en minä päästä sinua. Niin hän nousi ja seurasi häntä.
verses.king_james: And the mother of the child said, As the Lord liveth, and as thy soul liveth, I will not leave thee. And he arose, and followed her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Giezi autem præcesserat ante eos, et posuerat baculum super faciem pueri, et non erat vox, neque sensus : reversusque est in occursum ejus, et nuntiavit ei, dicens : Non surrexit puer.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְגֵחֲזִ֞י עָבַ֣ר לִפְנֵיהֶ֗ם וַיָּ֤שֶׂם אֶת־הַמִּשְׁעֶ֙נֶת֙ עַל־פְּנֵ֣י הַנַּ֔עַר וְאֵ֥ין ק֖וֹל וְאֵ֣ין קָ֑שֶׁב וַיָּ֤שׇׁב לִקְרָאתוֹ֙ וַיַּגֶּד־ל֣וֹ לֵאמֹ֔ר לֹ֥א הֵקִ֖יץ הַנָּֽעַר׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Giezi was gone before them, and laid the staff upon the face of the child, and there was no voice nor sense: and he returned to meet him, and told him, saying: The child is not risen.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Gehatsi meni pois heidän edellänsä, ja laski sauvansa pojan kasvoille, vaan ei ollut ääntä eikä tuntoa. Niin hän palasi ja kohtasi hänen, ilmoitti hänelle ja sanoi: ei poika herännyt.
verses.king_james: And Gehazi passed on before them, and laid the staff upon the face of the child; but there was neither voice, nor hearing. Wherefore he went again to meet him, and told him, saying, The child is not awaked.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ingressus est ergo Eliseus domum, et ecce puer mortuus jacebat in lectulo ejus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֥א אֱלִישָׁ֖ע הַבָּ֑יְתָה וְהִנֵּ֤ה הַנַּ֙עַר֙ מֵ֔ת מֻשְׁכָּ֖ב עַל־מִטָּתֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Eliseus, therefore, went into the house, and behold the child lay dead on his bed:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin Elisa tuli huoneesen, katso, poika makasi kuolleena hänen vuoteellansa.
verses.king_james: And when Elisha was come into the house, behold, the child was dead, and laid upon his bed.

verses.latin_vulgate: ingressusque clausit ostium super se et super puerum, et oravit ad Dominum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֕א וַיִּסְגֹּ֥ר הַדֶּ֖לֶת בְּעַ֣ד שְׁנֵיהֶ֑ם וַיִּתְפַּלֵּ֖ל אֶל־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And going in, he shut the door upon him, and upon the child, and prayed to the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän meni sisälle ja sulki oven molempain perään; ja hän rukoili Herraa,
verses.king_james: He went in therefore, and shut the door upon them twain, and prayed unto the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ascendit, et incubuit super puerum : posuitque os suum super os ejus, et oculos suos super oculos ejus, et manus suas super manus ejus : et incurvavit se super eum, et calefacta est caro pueri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֜עַל וַיִּשְׁכַּ֣ב עַל־הַיֶּ֗לֶד וַיָּ֩שֶׂם֩ פִּ֨יו עַל־פִּ֜יו וְעֵינָ֤יו עַל־עֵינָיו֙ וְכַפָּ֣יו עַל־כַּפָּ֔ו וַיִּגְהַ֖ר עָלָ֑יו וַיָּ֖חׇם בְּשַׂ֥ר הַיָּֽלֶד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he went up, and lay upon the child: and put his mouth upon his mouth, and his eyes upon his eyes, and his hands upon his hands: and he bowed himself upon him, and the child's flesh grew warm.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nousi ja laski itsensä lapsen päälle, ja pani suunsa hänen suunsa päälle, ja silmänsä hänen silmäinsä päälle, ja kätensä hänen kättensä päälle, ja niin ojensi itsensä hänen päällensä, että lapsen ruumis tuli lämpimäksi.
verses.king_james: And he went up, and lay upon the child, and put his mouth upon his mouth, and his eyes upon his eyes, and his hands upon his hands: and he stretched himself upon the child; and the flesh of the child waxed warm.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille reversus, deambulavit in domo, semel huc atque illuc : et ascendit, et incubuit super eum : et oscitavit puer septies, aperuitque oculos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֜שׇׁב וַיֵּ֣לֶךְ בַּבַּ֗יִת אַחַ֥ת הֵ֙נָּה֙ וְאַחַ֣ת הֵ֔נָּה וַיַּ֖עַל וַיִּגְהַ֣ר עָלָ֑יו וַיְזוֹרֵ֤ר הַנַּ֙עַר֙ עַד־שֶׁ֣בַע פְּעָמִ֔ים וַיִּפְקַ֥ח הַנַּ֖עַר אֶת־עֵינָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then he returned and walked in the house, once to and fro: and he went up, and lay upon him: and the child gaped seven times, and opened his eyes.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän nousi jälleen ja kävi kerran sinne ja tänne huoneessa, nousi ja laski itsensä hänen päällensä. Niin poika aivasti seitsemän kertaa, sitte avasi poika silmänsä.
verses.king_james: Then he returned, and walked in the house to and fro; and went up, and stretched himself upon him: and the child sneezed seven times, and the child opened his eyes.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille vocavit Giezi, et dixit ei : Voca Sunamitidem hanc. Quæ vocata, ingressa est ad eum. Qui ait : Tolle filium tuum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְרָ֣א אֶל־גֵּיחֲזִ֗י וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ קְרָא֙ אֶל־הַשֻּׁנַמִּ֣ית הַזֹּ֔את וַיִּקְרָאֶ֖הָ וַתָּבֹ֣א אֵלָ֑יו וַיֹּ֖אמֶר שְׂאִ֥י בְנֵֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he called Giezi, and said to him: Call this Sunamitess. And she being called, went in to him: and he said: Take up thy son.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän kutsui Gehatsin ja sanoi: kutsu Sunemin vaimo. Ja kuin hän oli hänen kutsunut, tuli hän sisälle hänen tykönsä. Hän sanoi: ota poikas!
verses.king_james: And he called Gehazi, and said, Call this Shunammite. So he called her. And when she was come in unto him, he said, Take up thy son.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit illa, et corruit ad pedes ejus, et adoravit super terram : tulitque filium suum, et egressa est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתָּבֹא֙ וַתִּפֹּ֣ל עַל־רַגְלָ֔יו וַתִּשְׁתַּ֖חוּ אָ֑רְצָה וַתִּשָּׂ֥א אֶת־בְּנָ֖הּ וַתֵּצֵֽא׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: She came and fell at his feet, and worshipped upon the ground: and took up her son, and went out.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin hän tuli ja lankesi hänen jalkainsa juureen, kumarsi itsensä maahan, ja otti poikansa ja meni ulos.
verses.king_james: Then she went in, and fell at his feet, and bowed herself to the ground, and took up her son, and went out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Eliseus reversus est in Galgala. Erat autem fames in terra, et filii prophetarum habitabant coram eo. Dixitque uni de pueris suis : Pone ollam grandem, et coque pulmentum filiis prophetarum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וֶאֱלִישָׁ֞ע שָׁ֤ב הַגִּלְגָּ֙לָה֙ וְהָרָעָ֣ב בָּאָ֔רֶץ וּבְנֵי֙ הַנְּבִיאִ֔ים יֹשְׁבִ֖ים לְפָנָ֑יו וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לְנַעֲר֗וֹ שְׁפֹת֙ הַסִּ֣יר הַגְּדוֹלָ֔ה וּבַשֵּׁ֥ל נָזִ֖יד לִבְנֵ֥י הַנְּבִיאִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Eliseus returned to Galgal, and there was a famine in the land, and the sons of the prophets dwelt before him: And he said to one of his servants: Set on the great pot, and boil pottage for the sons of the prophets.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Elisa palasi Gilgaliin, ja kallis aika oli maalla, ja prophetain pojat olivat hänen edessänsä. Ja hän sanoi palveliallensa: ota suuri pata ja keitä lientä prophetain pojille.
verses.king_james: And Elisha came again to Gilgal: and there was a dearth in the land; and the sons of the prophets were sitting before him: and he said unto his servant, Set on the great pot, and seethe pottage for the sons of the prophets.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et egressus est unus in agrum ut colligeret herbas agrestes : invenitque quasi vitem silvestrem, et collegit ex ea colocynthidas agri, et implevit pallium suum, et reversus concidit in ollam pulmenti : nesciebat enim quid esset.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּצֵ֨א אֶחָ֣ד אֶל־הַשָּׂדֶה֮ לְלַקֵּ֣ט אֹרֹת֒ וַיִּמְצָא֙ גֶּ֣פֶן שָׂדֶ֔ה וַיְלַקֵּ֥ט מִמֶּ֛נּוּ פַּקֻּעֹ֥ת שָׂדֶ֖ה מְלֹ֣א בִגְד֑וֹ וַיָּבֹ֗א וַיְפַלַּ֛ח אֶל־סִ֥יר הַנָּזִ֖יד כִּי־לֹ֥א יָדָֽעוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And one went out into the field to gather wild herbs: and he found something like a wild vine, and gathered of it wild gourds of the field, and filled his mantle, and coming back, he shred them into the pot of pottage; for he knew not what it was.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin yksi läksi kedolle hakemaan ruohoja, ja löysi metsäviikunapuun, ja haki siitä kolokinteja vaatteensa täyden. Ja kuin hän tuli, leikkasi hän sen liemipataan; sillä ei he tunteneet sitä.
verses.king_james: And one went out into the field to gather herbs, and found a wild vine, and gathered thereof wild gourds his lap full, and came and shred them into the pot of pottage: for they knew them not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Infuderunt ergo sociis ut comederent : cumque gustassent de coctione, clamaverunt, dicentes : Mors in olla, vir Dei. Et non potuerunt comedere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּֽצְק֥וּ לַאֲנָשִׁ֖ים לֶאֱכ֑וֹל וַ֠יְהִ֠י כְּאׇכְלָ֨ם מֵהַנָּזִ֜יד וְהֵ֣מָּה צָעָ֗קוּ וַיֹּֽאמְרוּ֙ מָ֤וֶת בַּסִּיר֙ אִ֣ישׁ הָאֱלֹהִ֔ים וְלֹ֥א יָכְל֖וּ לֶאֱכֹֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they poured it out for their companions to eat: and when they had tasted of the pottage, they cried out, saying: Death is in the pot, O man of God. And they could not eat thereof.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin he sen antoivat miesten eteen syödä, söivät he liemestä ja huusivat, sanoen: Jumalan mies, kuolema on padassa! Sillä ei he saaneet syödä.
verses.king_james: So they poured out for the men to eat. And it came to pass, as they were eating of the pottage, that they cried out, and said, O thou man of God, there is death in the pot. And they could not eat thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille : Afferte, inquit, farinam. Cumque tulissent, misit in ollam, et ait : Infunde turbæ, ut comedant. Et non fuit amplius quidquam amaritudinis in olla.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ וּקְחוּ־קֶ֔מַח וַיַּשְׁלֵ֖ךְ אֶל־הַסִּ֑יר וַיֹּ֗אמֶר צַ֤ק לָעָם֙ וְיֹאכֵ֔לוּ וְלֹ֥א הָיָ֛ה דָּבָ֥ר רָ֖ע בַּסִּֽיר׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: But he said: Bring some meal. And when they had brought it, he cast it into the pot, and said: Pour out for the people, that they may eat. And there was now no bitterness in the pot.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi: tuokaat tänne jauhoja, ja hän heitti pataan ja sanoi: antakaat kansalle syödä, ja ei ollut mitään vahinkoa padassa.
verses.king_james: But he said, Then bring meal. And he cast it into the pot; and he said, Pour out for the people, that they may eat. And there was no harm in the pot.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vir autem quidam venit de Baalsalisa deferens viro Dei panes primitiarum, viginti panes hordeaceos, et frumentum novum in pera sua. At ille dixit : Da populo, ut comedat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִ֨ישׁ בָּ֜א מִבַּ֣עַל שָׁלִ֗שָׁה וַיָּבֵא֩ לְאִ֨ישׁ הָאֱלֹהִ֜ים לֶ֤חֶם בִּכּוּרִים֙ עֶשְׂרִֽים־לֶ֣חֶם שְׂעֹרִ֔ים וְכַרְמֶ֖ל בְּצִקְלֹנ֑וֹ וַיֹּ֕אמֶר תֵּ֥ן לָעָ֖ם וְיֹאכֵֽלוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And a certain man came from Baalsalisa, bringing to the man of God, bread of the firstfruits, twenty loaves of barley, and new corn in his scrip. And he said: Give to the people, that they may eat.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja yksi mies tuli Baalsalisasta ja kantoi Jumalan miehelle uutisleipiä, kaksikymmentä ohraista leipää ja uutisjyviä vaatteissansa. Mutta hän sanoi: anna kansalle, että he söisivät.
verses.king_james: And there came a man from Baal–shalisha, and brought the man of God bread of the firstfruits, twenty loaves of barley, and full ears of corn in the husk thereof. And he said, Give unto the people, that they may eat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responditque ei minister ejus : Quantum est hoc, ut apponam centum viris ? Rursum ille ait : Da populo, ut comedat : hæc enim dicit Dominus : Comedent, et supererit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ מְשָׁ֣רְת֔וֹ מָ֚ה אֶתֵּ֣ן זֶ֔ה לִפְנֵ֖י מֵ֣אָה אִ֑ישׁ וַיֹּ֗אמֶר תֵּ֤ן לָעָם֙ וְיֹאכֵ֔לוּ כִּ֣י כֹ֥ה אָמַ֛ר יְהֹוָ֖ה אָכֹ֥ל וְהוֹתֵֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And his servant answered him: How much is this, that I should set it before a hundred men? He said again: Give to the people, that they may eat: for thus saith the Lord: They shall eat, and there shall be left.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänen palveliansa sanoi: mitä minä siitä annan sadalle miehelle? Hän sanoi: anna kansalle syödä; sillä näin sanoo Herra: he syövät ja vielä sittenkin jää.
verses.king_james: And his servitor said, What, should I set this before an hundred men? He said again, Give the people, that they may eat: for thus saith the Lord, They shall eat, and shall leave thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Posuit itaque coram eis : qui comederunt, et superfuit juxta verbum Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתֵּ֧ן לִפְנֵיהֶ֛ם וַיֹּאכְל֥וּ וַיּוֹתִ֖רוּ כִּדְבַ֥ר יְהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: So he set it before them: and they ate, and there was left, according to the word of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän pani ne heidän eteensä, ja he söivät; ja vielä oli liiaksi Herran sanan jälkeen.
verses.king_james: So he set it before them, and they did eat, and left thereof, according to the word of the Lord.
